holidays.name: World Vegetarian Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate vegetarianism and the benefits of a plant based diet.
holidays.funFact: Vegetarians make up about 8 percent of the global population, with India having the highest percentage of vegetarians in the world.
holidays.shareText: Going green today for World Vegetarian Day! Plants are powerful. 🥦 #WorldVegetarianDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Architecture Day
holidays.description: A global celebration of architecture and the built environment that shapes our world.
holidays.funFact: The Eiffel Tower can grow up to 6 inches taller in summer due to thermal expansion of its iron structure.
holidays.shareText: Marveling at the structures around me on World Architecture Day! 🏛️ #WorldArchitectureDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Fried Scallops Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy the crispy golden goodness of fried scallops, a beloved seafood treat.
holidays.funFact: Scallops are one of the few bivalves that can swim by rapidly clapping their shells together to escape predators.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the crispy perfection of fried scallops today! 🍤 #NationalFriedScallopsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Habitat Day
holidays.description: A United Nations designated day to reflect on the state of human settlements and the right to adequate shelter.
holidays.funFact: More than 1 billion people worldwide live in inadequate housing, which is why World Habitat Day was established in 1985.
holidays.shareText: Everyone deserves a safe place to call home. Thinking about World Habitat Day today. 🏘️ #WorldHabitatDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Caramel Custard Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to the silky, sweet delight of caramel custard, also known as flan in many cultures.
holidays.funFact: Caramel custard dates back to ancient Rome, where eggs and milk were combined with honey to create early versions of this dessert.
holidays.shareText: Diving into a dreamy caramel custard today! 🍮 #NationalCaramelCustardDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Smile Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to spreading kindness and smiles around the world, inspired by Harvey Ball who created the smiley face.
holidays.funFact: Harvey Ball created the original smiley face in 1963 in just 10 minutes, earning only $45 for his iconic design.
holidays.shareText: Spreading smiles everywhere today! 😊 #WorldSmileDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cinnamon Roll Day
holidays.description: A sweet celebration of the beloved cinnamon roll, a warm and gooey pastry enjoyed worldwide.
holidays.funFact: Sweden holds Cinnamon Roll Day on October 4th as well, and Swedes consume about 316 cinnamon rolls per person each year.
holidays.shareText: Is there anything better than a fresh warm cinnamon roll? 🥐 #NationalCinnamonRollDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Animal Day
holidays.description: An international day of action for animal rights and welfare, raising awareness for animals worldwide.
holidays.funFact: World Animal Day was first celebrated on March 24, 1925 in Berlin, but was later moved to October 4th to coincide with the feast day of Saint Francis of Assisi.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating every creature great and small on World Animal Day! 🐾 #WorldAnimalDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Teachers Day
holidays.description: A UNESCO designated day honoring teachers and recognizing the vital role they play in education and society.
holidays.funFact: There are approximately 85 million teachers worldwide, and World Teachers Day has been celebrated since 1994.
holidays.shareText: Thank you to every teacher who made a difference! 📚 #WorldTeachersDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Apple Betty Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy Apple Betty, a classic American baked dessert made with apples and a spiced breadcrumb topping.
holidays.funFact: Apple Betty is one of the oldest American desserts, with recipes dating back to colonial times in the 1700s.
holidays.shareText: Baking up a cozy Apple Betty to celebrate this fall favorite! 🍎 #NationalAppleBettyDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Noodle Day
holidays.description: A day to slurp, twirl, and celebrate noodles in all their glorious forms from around the world.
holidays.funFact: The oldest noodles ever found were 4000 years old, discovered in China in 2005, made from millet grass.
holidays.shareText: Noodles are life and today is their day! 🍜 #NationalNoodleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Mad Hatter Day
holidays.description: A whimsical day inspired by the Mad Hatter from Alice in Wonderland, celebrated with silliness and nonsense.
holidays.funFact: The phrase mad as a hatter originated from the 18th and 19th centuries when hat makers used mercury in felt processing, which caused neurological symptoms.
holidays.shareText: Have a very merry unbirthday on Mad Hatter Day! 🎩 #MadHatterDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Frappe Day
holidays.description: A day to celebrate the frappe, a chilled blended coffee drink that is creamy, sweet, and refreshing.
holidays.funFact: The modern frappe was invented in 1957 in Thessaloniki, Greece by a Nescafe representative who blended instant coffee with cold water and ice.
holidays.shareText: Blending up something cold and delicious for National Frappe Day! 🥤 #NationalFrappeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Kale Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating kale, the nutrient packed leafy green that has become a superfood sensation.
holidays.funFact: Kale was one of the most popular vegetables in Europe during the Middle Ages and into World War II, when it was grown extensively in Britain.
holidays.shareText: Kale yeah! Celebrating the king of greens today. 🥬 #NationalKaleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pierogi Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the pierogi, a beloved Eastern European dumpling stuffed with savory or sweet fillings.
holidays.funFact: Pierogies are the national dish of Poland, and Canadians eat so many of them that Canada has been called the pierogi capital of North America.
holidays.shareText: Stuffed with happiness on National Pierogi Day! 🥟 #NationalPierogiDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Octopus Day
holidays.description: A day to appreciate the octopus, one of the most intelligent and fascinating creatures in the ocean.
holidays.funFact: Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and nine brains. Each of their eight arms has its own mini brain that can act independently.
holidays.shareText: Eight arms of appreciation for World Octopus Day! 🐙 #WorldOctopusDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Moldy Cheese Day
holidays.description: A quirky food holiday celebrating the funky, flavorful world of mold ripened cheeses like blue cheese and brie.
holidays.funFact: The blue veins in blue cheese are created by injecting the cheese with Penicillium mold spores using long needles, then allowing air to circulate through the holes.
holidays.shareText: Embracing the funk on National Moldy Cheese Day! 🧀 #NationalMoldyCheeseDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Post Day
holidays.description: An annual celebration of the postal service and its role in connecting people across the globe.
holidays.funFact: The Universal Postal Union, which oversees World Post Day, was founded in 1874 and is one of the oldest international organizations in the world.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the wonder of mail and connection on World Post Day! 📬 #WorldPostDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Mental Health Day
holidays.description: A global awareness day to educate, raise awareness, and advocate against the social stigma of mental health issues.
holidays.funFact: World Mental Health Day was first observed in 1992, and the World Health Organization estimates that 1 in 4 people worldwide will be affected by mental illness at some point.
holidays.shareText: Mental health matters every single day. You are not alone. 🧠 #WorldMentalHealthDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cake Decorating Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the art and creativity of cake decorating, from simple frosting swirls to elaborate fondant sculptures.
holidays.funFact: The most expensive cake ever made cost $75 million and was decorated with 4000 diamonds, created by a British jeweler.
holidays.shareText: Making every slice a masterpiece on National Cake Decorating Day! 🎂 #NationalCakeDecoratingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Sausage Pizza Day
holidays.description: A day to savor the classic combination of savory sausage on a perfectly baked pizza.
holidays.funFact: Sausage is the second most popular pizza topping in the United States, just behind pepperoni, and Americans consume approximately 3 billion pizzas per year.
holidays.shareText: Sausage pizza is having its moment and I am here for it! 🍕 #NationalSausagePizzaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Day of the Girl Child
holidays.description: A United Nations day recognizing girls rights and the unique challenges girls face around the world.
holidays.funFact: The International Day of the Girl Child was declared by the United Nations in 2011 and first observed on October 11, 2012.
holidays.shareText: Championing the dreams and rights of girls everywhere today! 👧 #DayOfTheGirl #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Gumbo Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating gumbo, the hearty Louisiana stew that is a cornerstone of Creole and Cajun cuisine.
holidays.funFact: The word gumbo is derived from the West African word for okra, ki ngombo, reflecting the dish's African culinary roots.
holidays.shareText: Stirring up something special for National Gumbo Day! 🍲 #NationalGumboDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Indigenous Peoples Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the history, culture, and contributions of Indigenous peoples across the Americas and beyond.
holidays.funFact: South Dakota was the first US state to officially replace Columbus Day with Native American Day back in 1990.
holidays.shareText: Honoring the rich cultures and enduring strength of Indigenous peoples today. 🪶 #IndigenousPeoplesDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pumpkin Cheesecake Day
holidays.description: A day dedicated to the perfect autumn dessert mashup: creamy cheesecake infused with spiced pumpkin flavor.
holidays.funFact: Pumpkin is technically a fruit, not a vegetable, because it contains seeds and develops from a flowering plant.
holidays.shareText: Fall flavors and creamy dreams collide on National Pumpkin Cheesecake Day! 🎃 #NationalPumpkinCheesecakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Day for Failure
holidays.description: A day to embrace failure as a necessary part of learning, growth, and eventual success.
holidays.funFact: Thomas Edison reportedly failed over 1000 times before successfully inventing the light bulb, saying each attempt taught him something new.
holidays.shareText: Here is to every stumble that taught us something! 🙃 #InternationalDayForFailure #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Dessert Day
holidays.description: A sweet celebration of all things dessert, from cakes and cookies to ice cream and pies.
holidays.funFact: The word dessert comes from the French word desservir, meaning to clear the table, as it was traditionally served after the table was cleared.
holidays.shareText: Life is short so eat dessert first! 🍰 #NationalDessertDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Fossil Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating paleontology and the remarkable window into Earths history provided by fossils.
holidays.funFact: The oldest known fossils are approximately 3.5 billion years old and are microscopic bacteria found in Western Australia.
holidays.shareText: Digging into Earths ancient past on National Fossil Day! 🦕 #NationalFossilDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Cheese Curd Day
holidays.description: A day honoring cheese curds, the fresh squeaky bites of cheddar cheese that are a Midwest favorite.
holidays.funFact: Fresh cheese curds squeak when you bite into them because of long protein strands rubbing against tooth enamel, a quality that fades within 12 hours of being made.
holidays.shareText: Listen for the squeak on National Cheese Curd Day! 🧀 #NationalCheeseCurdDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Global Handwashing Day
holidays.description: A worldwide advocacy day dedicated to increasing awareness of the importance of handwashing with soap.
holidays.funFact: Handwashing with soap can reduce diarrheal disease risk by up to 50 percent and respiratory infections by 16 percent, making it one of the most effective public health interventions.
holidays.shareText: Clean hands save lives! Scrub up for Global Handwashing Day. 🧼 #GlobalHandwashingDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Food Day
holidays.description: A United Nations day commemorating the founding of the Food and Agriculture Organization, raising awareness of global hunger.
holidays.funFact: Approximately 828 million people worldwide go hungry each day, yet one third of all food produced globally is wasted.
holidays.shareText: Food is a human right. Standing with the hungry on World Food Day. 🌾 #WorldFoodDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Boss Day
holidays.description: A day to recognize and appreciate great bosses and the leadership they provide in the workplace.
holidays.funFact: National Boss Day was created in 1958 by Patricia Bays Haroski, who registered it with the US Chamber of Commerce to honor her father, who was also her boss.
holidays.shareText: Shoutout to the leaders who inspire and support their teams! 💼 #NationalBossDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Pasta Day
holidays.description: A delicious day to celebrate pasta in all its wonderful shapes, sauces, and preparations.
holidays.funFact: There are over 600 different pasta shapes produced worldwide, and Italians eat an average of 51 pounds of pasta per person per year.
holidays.shareText: Every shape, every sauce, every bite is worth celebrating on National Pasta Day! 🍝 #NationalPastaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Day for the Eradication of Poverty
holidays.description: A United Nations observance day calling for action to end poverty and promote social justice worldwide.
holidays.funFact: Since 1990, more than one billion people have been lifted out of extreme poverty, yet nearly 700 million people still live on less than $1.90 per day.
holidays.shareText: No one should be left behind. Fighting for a world without poverty. 🤝 #EndPoverty #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chocolate Cupcake Day
holidays.description: A day to indulge in rich, moist chocolate cupcakes topped with fluffy frosting.
holidays.funFact: The cupcake got its name in the early 1800s because ingredients were measured in cups rather than by weight, making the recipe simple to remember.
holidays.shareText: Small but mighty, just like chocolate cupcakes! 🧁 #NationalChocolateCupcakeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Menopause Day
holidays.description: A day raising awareness about menopause and the support options available for those experiencing it.
holidays.funFact: Menopause is a natural biological process that typically occurs in a persons late 40s to early 50s, and it is the only health transition experienced exclusively by roughly half the worlds population.
holidays.shareText: Breaking the silence and building awareness on World Menopause Day. 💜 #WorldMenopauseDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Seafood Bisque Day
holidays.description: A day to savor the creamy, rich elegance of seafood bisque, a classic French inspired soup.
holidays.funFact: Bisque is a smooth, creamy soup that was originally made from crustaceans, with the term bisque possibly derived from the Bay of Biscay off the coasts of France and Spain.
holidays.shareText: Warming up with a bowl of velvety seafood bisque today! 🦐 #NationalSeafoodBisqueDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Evaluate Your Life Day
holidays.description: A lighthearted day set aside to pause and reflect on your goals, choices, and overall direction in life.
holidays.funFact: Studies show that people who regularly reflect on their lives and set intentional goals are significantly more likely to report greater life satisfaction.
holidays.shareText: Taking a moment to ask the big questions on Evaluate Your Life Day! 🤔 #EvaluateYourLifeDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Brandied Fruit Day
holidays.description: A day to enjoy the sweet and boozy tradition of fruit preserved in brandy, a classic homemade treat.
holidays.funFact: Brandied fruits were historically used as a way to preserve seasonal fruits before refrigeration, and rumtopf, a German tradition, involves layering fruits and rum all summer long.
holidays.shareText: Raising a jar of brandied fruit for this deliciously old fashioned holiday! 🍑 #NationalBrandiedFruitDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Statistics Day
holidays.description: A United Nations day promoting the work of national and international statistical systems and celebrating data.
holidays.funFact: The first World Statistics Day was celebrated on October 20, 2010, with the date chosen because it reads 20.10.2010, forming a numerical palindrome.
holidays.shareText: The numbers never lie on World Statistics Day! 📊 #WorldStatisticsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Apple Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the beloved apple, one of the most widely grown and consumed fruits in the world.
holidays.funFact: There are over 7500 varieties of apples grown worldwide, and it would take more than 20 years to try a different variety every day.
holidays.shareText: An apple a day and a whole lot of celebrating on National Apple Day! 🍎 #NationalAppleDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Count Your Buttons Day
holidays.description: A whimsical day encouraging people to notice, collect, and celebrate buttons of all shapes and sizes.
holidays.funFact: The oldest known buttons date back over 5000 years and were found in the Indus Valley, originally used as ornamental objects rather than fasteners.
holidays.shareText: Button button who has the button? Counting mine today! 🔘 #CountYourButtonsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Nut Day
holidays.description: A crunchy celebration of nuts of all kinds, from almonds and walnuts to cashews and pecans.
holidays.funFact: Peanuts are not actually nuts. They are legumes that grow underground, making them more closely related to beans and lentils than to tree nuts.
holidays.shareText: Going nuts for National Nut Day! 🥜 #NationalNutDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: International Caps Lock Day
holidays.description: A humorous day poking fun at the overuse of caps lock online and celebrating typed shouty enthusiasm.
holidays.funFact: The caps lock key was inherited from the typewriter era where a shift lock key physically locked the shift mechanism, a design choice that has persisted despite many calls to remove it.
holidays.shareText: TODAY WE CELEBRATE WITH FULL ENTHUSIASM FOR INTERNATIONAL CAPS LOCK DAY! ⬆️ #CapsLockDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Boston Cream Pie Day
holidays.description: A day honoring Bostons official dessert, a sponge cake filled with custard and topped with chocolate glaze.
holidays.funFact: Boston Cream Pie was invented at the Parker House Hotel in Boston in 1856 and became Massachusetts official state dessert in 1996.
holidays.shareText: Layers of happiness on National Boston Cream Pie Day! 🍰 #NationalBostonCreamPieDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: TV Talk Show Host Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the charismatic hosts who have entertained and informed audiences through the art of the talk show.
holidays.funFact: The Tonight Show, one of the longest running talk shows, first aired on September 27, 1954, making it over 70 years old.
holidays.shareText: Celebrating the hosts who make us laugh, think, and feel seen! 🎙️ #TVTalkShowHostDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Bologna Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the classic American lunchbox staple, bologna, the beloved processed meat sandwich hero.
holidays.funFact: American bologna is a descendant of the Italian mortadella, which originated in Bologna, Italy, and must contain at least 15 percent pork fat by law.
holidays.shareText: My bologna has a first name and it starts with celebration! 🥪 #NationalBolognaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: United Nations Day
holidays.description: A day marking the anniversary of the United Nations Charter coming into force in 1945, celebrating global cooperation.
holidays.funFact: The United Nations was founded in 1945 with 51 member states. Today it has 193 member countries, representing nearly every nation on Earth.
holidays.shareText: One world, many voices. Marking United Nations Day with hope for peace. 🌐 #UnitedNationsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Greasy Foods Day
holidays.description: A gloriously indulgent day giving permission to enjoy all the fried, greasy, guilty pleasure foods you love.
holidays.funFact: The Maillard reaction is the chemical process responsible for the delicious browning and flavor of fried foods, occurring when amino acids and sugars react at high temperatures.
holidays.shareText: No guilt allowed on National Greasy Foods Day! Indulge away. 🍟 #NationalGreasyFoodsDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Pasta Day
holidays.description: An international celebration of pasta in all its forms, bringing together pasta lovers from around the globe.
holidays.funFact: World Pasta Day was established in 1995 at the first World Pasta Congress in Rome, where 40 pasta producers from around the world gathered to celebrate.
holidays.shareText: Double the pasta celebrations this month on World Pasta Day! 🍝 #WorldPastaDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Mincemeat Day
holidays.description: A day honoring the rich, spiced filling used in traditional British mince pies, a blend of dried fruits and warming spices.
holidays.funFact: Originally mincemeat did contain real minced meat mixed with fruits and spices, a medieval method of preservation, but over centuries the meat was gradually replaced with suet and then butter.
holidays.shareText: A taste of tradition on National Mincemeat Day! 🥧 #NationalMincemeatDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Pumpkin Day
holidays.description: A festive autumn day celebrating pumpkins, the iconic orange gourds of the fall season.
holidays.funFact: The largest pumpkin ever recorded weighed 2749 pounds and was grown in Belgium in 2021, roughly the size of a small car.
holidays.shareText: Patch perfect and orange in every way on Pumpkin Day! 🎃 #PumpkinDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National American Beer Day
holidays.description: A day raising a glass to American craft and commercial beer and the rich brewing culture of the United States.
holidays.funFact: The United States has more than 9000 craft breweries, making it the country with the most breweries in the world by a significant margin.
holidays.shareText: Cheers to American ingenuity and great beer on National American Beer Day! 🍺 #NationalAmericanBeerDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Cranky Coworkers Day
holidays.description: A humorous day acknowledging workplace grumpiness and encouraging a little extra patience and kindness at the office.
holidays.funFact: According to workplace surveys, Monday mornings and the period right before lunch are statistically the peak times for workplace irritability.
holidays.shareText: Extra patience for everyone today on Cranky Coworkers Day! 😤 #CrankyCoworkersDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Chocolate Day
holidays.description: A day to indulge in all things chocolate, from dark and milk to white chocolate in every form imaginable.
holidays.funFact: It takes approximately 400 cocoa beans to make a single pound of chocolate, and the average American eats about 9 pounds of chocolate per year.
holidays.shareText: Life is better with chocolate and today proves it! 🍫 #NationalChocolateDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Plush Animal Lovers Day
holidays.description: A cozy day celebrating the beloved stuffed animals and plush toys that bring comfort and joy to people of all ages.
holidays.funFact: The teddy bear was named after President Theodore Roosevelt in 1902 after a story about him refusing to shoot a bear that had been tied to a tree during a hunting trip.
holidays.shareText: Hugging my favorites extra tight on Plush Animal Lovers Day! 🧸 #PlushAnimalLoversDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Oatmeal Day
holidays.description: A wholesome day celebrating oatmeal, the hearty and nutritious breakfast staple enjoyed around the world.
holidays.funFact: Oats are one of the few grains that contain a unique type of fiber called beta glucan, which has been clinically shown to help lower cholesterol levels.
holidays.shareText: Starting the day right with a warm bowl of oatmeal! 🥣 #NationalOatmealDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: World Psoriasis Day
holidays.description: A global awareness day for those living with psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis, advocating for better care and understanding.
holidays.funFact: Psoriasis affects approximately 125 million people worldwide, about 2 to 3 percent of the global population, making it one of the most common chronic skin conditions.
holidays.shareText: Raising awareness and standing in solidarity on World Psoriasis Day. 💛 #WorldPsoriasisDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Candy Corn Day
holidays.description: A day celebrating the iconic tricolored Halloween candy that people either passionately love or strongly dislike.
holidays.funFact: Candy corn was invented in the 1880s by George Renninger of the Wunderle Candy Company, and about 35 million pounds are produced each year.
holidays.shareText: Team candy corn or team no candy corn? Either way it is the season! 🍬 #NationalCandyCornDay #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Haunted Refrigerator Night
holidays.description: A spooky fun holiday encouraging you to clean out your fridge and confront whatever mysterious leftovers haunt its shelves.
holidays.funFact: The average American refrigerator contains approximately 103 different types of bacteria on its surfaces, making that forgotten leftover even spookier.
holidays.shareText: Whatever is lurking in the back of the fridge has met its match tonight! 👻 #HauntedRefrigeratorNight #CelebriDay

holidays.name: Halloween
holidays.description: The beloved annual celebration of costumes, candy, and all things spooky, rooted in the ancient Celtic festival of Samhain.
holidays.funFact: Halloween is the second largest commercial holiday in the United States after Christmas, with Americans spending over 10 billion dollars each year on costumes, candy, and decorations.
holidays.shareText: Trick or treat! Wishing everyone a frightfully fun Halloween! 🎃 #Halloween #CelebriDay

holidays.name: National Caramel Apple Day
holidays.description: A perfect fall treat day celebrating the irresistible combination of crisp apples coated in rich caramel.
holidays.funFact: The caramel apple was invented by a Kraft Foods employee named Dan Walker in the 1950s when he experimented by dipping apples into melted caramel candies.
holidays.shareText: Sweet and sticky and absolutely worth it on National Caramel Apple Day! 🍎 #NationalCaramelAppleDay #CelebriDay
